verses.1: Ther is then no damnacion to them which are in Christ Iesu which walke not after the flesshe: but after the sprete.
verses.2: For the lawe of the sprete that bringeth life thorowe Iesus Christ hath delivered me from the lawe of synne and deeth.
verses.3: For what the lawe coulde not doo in as moche it was weake because of the flesshe: that performed God and sent his sonne in the similitude of synfull flesshe and by synne daned synne in the flesshe:
verses.4: that the rightewesnes requyred of the lawe myght be fulfilled in vs which walke not after the flesshe but after the sprete.
verses.5: For they that are carnall are carnally mynded. But they that are spirituall are gostly mynded.
verses.6: To be carnally mynded is deeth. But to be spiritually mynded is lyfe and peace.
verses.7: Because that the flesshly mynde is emnyte agaynst God: for it is not obedient to the lawe of God nether can be.
verses.8: So then they that are geven to the flesshe cannot please God.
verses.9: But ye are not geven to the flesshe but to the sprete: yf so be that the sprite of God dwell in you. If ther be eny man that hath not the sprite of Christ the same is none of his.
verses.10: Yf Christ be in you the body is deed because of synne: but the sprite is lyfe for rightewesnes sake.
verses.11: Wherfore if the sprite of him that raysed vppe Iesus from deeth dwell in you: even he that raysed vp Christ from deeth shall quycken youre mortall bodyes because that this sprite dwelleth in you.
verses.12: Therfore brethren we are nowe detters not to the flesshe to live after the flesshe.
verses.13: For if ye live after the flesshe ye must dye. But yf ye mortifie the dedes of the body by the helpe of the sprite ye shall lyve.
verses.14: For as many as are led by the sprete of God: they are the sonnes of god.
verses.15: For ye have not receaved the sprete of bondage to feare eny moare but ye have receaved the sprite of adopcion wherby we crye Abba father.
verses.16: The same sprete certifieth oure sprete that we are the sonnes of God.
verses.17: Yf we be sonnes we are also heyres the heyres I meane of God and heyres anexed with Christ: if so be that we suffer togedder that we maye be glorified to gedder.
verses.18: For I suppose that the affliccions of this lyfe are not worthy of the glory which shalbe shewed vpon vs.
verses.19: Also the fervent desyre of the creatures abideth lokynge when the sonnes of God shall appere
verses.20: because the creatures are subdued to vanyte agaynst their will: but for his will which subdueth them in hope.
verses.21: For the very creatures shalbe delivered from the bondage of corrupcion into the glorious lybertie of the sonnes of God.
verses.22: For we knowe that every creature groneth with vs also and travayleth in payne even vnto this tyme.
verses.23: Not they only but even we also which have the fyrst frutes of the sprite morne in oureselves and wayte for the (adopcio) and loke for the delivraunce of oure bodyes.
verses.24: For we are savyd by hope. But hope that is sene is no hope. For how can a man hope for that which he seyth?
verses.25: But and yf we hope for that we se not then do we with pacience abyde for it.
verses.26: Lyke wyse the sprite also helpeth oure infirmities. For we knowe not what to desyre as we ought: but the sprete maketh intercession mightely for vs with gronynges which cannot be expressid with tonge.
verses.27: And he that searcheth the hertes knoweth what is the meaninge of the sprite: for he maketh intercession for the sayntes accordinge to the pleasure of god.
verses.28: For we knowe that all thinges worke for the best vnto them that love God which also are called of purpose.
verses.29: For those which he knewe before he also ordeyned before that they shuld be lyke fassioned vnto the shape of his sonne that he myght be the fyrst begotten sonne amonge many brethren.
verses.30: Morover which he apoynted before them he also called. And which he called them also he iustified which he iustified them he also glorified.
verses.31: What shall we then saye vnto these thinges? yf god be on oure syde: who can be agaynst vs?
verses.32: which spared not his awne sonne but gave him for vs all: how shall he not with him geve vs all thinges also?
verses.33: Who shall laye eny thinge to the charge of goddes chosen? it is god that iustifieth:
verses.34: who then shall condempne? it is Christ which is deed the rather which is rysen agayne which is also on the ryght honde of God and maketh intercession for vs.
verses.35: Who shall seperate vs from the love of god? shall tribulacion? or anguysshe? or persecucion? other honger? other nakednesse? other parell? other swearde?
verses.36: As it is written: For thy sake are we kylled all daye longe and are counted as shepe apoynted to be slayne.
verses.37: Neverthelesse in all these thinges we overcome strongly thorow his helpe that loved vs.
verses.38: Ye and I am sure that nether deeth nether lyfe nether angels nor rule nether power nether thinges present nether thinges to come
verses.39: nether heyth nether loweth nether eny other creature shalbe able to departe vs from the love of God shewed in Christ Iesu oure lorde.

verses.1: Now then there is no condemnation to them that are in Christ Iesus, which walke not after the flesh, but after the Spirit.
verses.2: For the Lawe of the Spirite of life, which is in Christ Iesus, hath freed mee from the lawe of sinne and of death.
verses.3: For (that that was impossible to ye Lawe, in as much as it was weake, because of ye flesh) God sending his owne Sonne, in ye similitude of sinful flesh, and for sinne, condened sinne in the flesh,
verses.4: That that righteousnes of the Law might be fulfilled in vs, which walke not after ye flesh, but after the Spirit.
verses.5: For they that are after the flesh, sauour the things of the flesh: but they that are after the Spirit, the things of the Spirit.
verses.6: For the wisedome of the flesh is death: but the wisedome of the Spirit is life and peace,
verses.7: Because the wisedome of the flesh is enimitie against God: for it is not subiect to the Lawe of God, neither in deede can be.
verses.8: So then they that are in the flesh, can not please God.
verses.9: Now ye are not in the flesh, but in ye Spirit, because ye spirit of God dwelleth in you: but if any man hath not ye Spirit of Christ, ye same is not his.
verses.10: And if Christ bee in you, the body is dead, because of sinne: but the Spirite is life for righteousnesse sake.
verses.11: But if the Spirit of him that raised vp Iesus from the dead, dwell in you, he that raised vp Christ from the dead, shall also quicken your mortall bodies, by his Spirit that dwelleth in you.
verses.12: Therefore brethren, wee are detters not to the flesh, to liue after the flesh:
verses.13: For if ye liue after the flesh, ye shall die: but if yee mortifie the deedes of the body by the Spirit, ye shall liue.
verses.14: For as many as are ledde by the Spirit of God, they are the sonnes of God.
verses.15: For ye haue not receiued the Spirit of bodage, to feare againe: but ye haue receiued the Spirit of adoption, whereby we cry Abba, Father.
verses.16: The same Spirit beareth witnesse with our spirit, that we are the children of God.
verses.17: If we be children, we are also heires, euen the heires of God, and heires annexed with Christ: if so be that we suffer with him, that we may also be glorified with him.
verses.18: For I count that the afflictions of this present time are not worthy of the glory, which shalbe shewed vnto vs.
verses.19: For the feruent desire of the creature waiteth when the sonnes of God shalbe reueiled,
verses.20: Because the creature is subiect to vanitie, not of it owne will, but by reason of him, which hath subdued it vnder hope,
verses.21: Because the creature also shall be deliuered from the bondage of corruption into the glorious libertie of the sonnes of God.
verses.22: For we knowe that euery creature groneth with vs also, and trauaileth in paine together vnto this present.
verses.23: And not onely the creature, but we also which haue the first fruites of the Spirit, euen we doe sigh in our selues, waiting for the adoption, euen the redemption of our body.
verses.24: For we are saued by hope: but hope that is seene, is not hope: for how can a man hope for that which he seeth?
verses.25: But if we hope for that we see not, we doe with patience abide for it.
verses.26: Likewise the Spirit also helpeth our infirmities: for we knowe not what to pray as wee ought: but the Spirit it selfe maketh request for vs with sighs, which cannot be expressed.
verses.27: But he that searcheth the heartes, knoweth what is the meaning of the Spirit: for he maketh request for ye Saints, according to the wil of God.
verses.28: Also we knowe that all thinges worke together for the best vnto them that loue God, euen to them that are called of his purpose.
verses.29: For those which hee knewe before, he also predestinate to bee made like to the image of his Sonne, that hee might be the first borne among many brethren.
verses.30: Moreouer whom he predestinate, them also he called, and whom he called, them also he iustified, and whom he iustified, them he also glorified.
verses.31: What shall we then say to these thinges? If God be on our side, who can be against vs?
verses.32: Who spared not his owne Sonne, but gaue him for vs all to death, how shall he not with him giue vs all things also?
verses.33: Who shall lay any thing to the charge of Gods chosen? it is God that iustifieth,
verses.34: Who shall condemne? it is Christ which is dead, yea, or rather, which is risen againe, who is also at the right hand of God, and maketh request also for vs.
verses.35: Who shall separate vs from the loue of Christ? shall tribulation or anguish, or persecution, or famine, or nakednesse, or perill, or sworde?
verses.36: As it is written, For thy sake are we killed all day long: we are counted as sheepe for the slaughter.
verses.37: Neuerthelesse, in all these thinges we are more then coquerours through him that loued vs.
verses.38: For I am perswaded that neither death, nor life, nor Angels, nor principalities, nor powers, nor things present, nor things to come,
verses.39: Nor height, nor depth, nor any other creature shalbe able to separate vs from the loue of God, which is in Christ Iesus our Lord.

verses.1: There is therefore now no condemnation to them which are in Christ Jesus, who walk not after the flesh, but after the Spirit.
verses.2: For the law of the Spirit of life in Christ Jesus hath made me free from the law of sin and death.
verses.3: For what the law could not do, in that it was weak through the flesh, God sending his own Son in the likeness of sinful flesh, and for sin, condemned sin in the flesh:
verses.4: That the righteousness of the law might be fulfilled in us, who walk not after the flesh, but after the Spirit.
verses.5: For they that are after the flesh do mind the things of the flesh; but they that are after the Spirit the things of the Spirit.
verses.6: For to be carnally minded is death; but to be spiritually minded is life and peace.
verses.7: Because the carnal mind is enmity against God: for it is not subject to the law of God, neither indeed can be.
verses.8: So then they that are in the flesh cannot please God.
verses.9: But ye are not in the flesh, but in the Spirit, if so be that the Spirit of God dwell in you. Now if any man have not the Spirit of Christ, he is none of his.
verses.10: And if Christ be in you, the body is dead because of sin; but the Spirit is life because of righteousness.
verses.11: But if the Spirit of him that raised up Jesus from the dead dwell in you, he that raised up Christ from the dead shall also quicken your mortal bodies by his Spirit that dwelleth in you.
verses.12: Therefore, brethren, we are debtors, not to the flesh, to live after the flesh.
verses.13: For if ye live after the flesh, ye shall die: but if ye through the Spirit do mortify the deeds of the body, ye shall live.
verses.14: For as many as are led by the Spirit of God, they are the sons of God.
verses.15: For ye have not received the spirit of bondage again to fear; but ye have received the Spirit of adoption, whereby we cry, Abba, Father.
verses.16: The Spirit itself beareth witness with our spirit, that we are the children of God:
verses.17: And if children, then heirs; heirs of God, and joint-heirs with Christ; if so be that we suffer with him, that we may be also glorified together.
verses.18: For I reckon that the sufferings of this present time are not worthy to be compared with the glory which shall be revealed in us.
verses.19: For the earnest expectation of the creature waiteth for the manifestation of the sons of God.
verses.20: For the creature was made subject to vanity, not willingly, but by reason of him who hath subjected the same in hope,
verses.21: Because the creature itself also shall be delivered from the bondage of corruption into the glorious liberty of the children of God.
verses.22: For we know that the whole creation groaneth and travaileth in pain together until now.
verses.23: And not only they, but ourselves also, which have the firstfruits of the Spirit, even we ourselves groan within ourselves, waiting for the adoption, to wit, the redemption of our body.
verses.24: For we are saved by hope: but hope that is seen is not hope: for what a man seeth, why doth he yet hope for?
verses.25: But if we hope for that we see not, then do we with patience wait for it.
verses.26: Likewise the Spirit also helpeth our infirmities: for we know not what we should pray for as we ought: but the Spirit itself maketh intercession for us with groanings which cannot be uttered.
verses.27: And he that searcheth the hearts knoweth what is the mind of the Spirit, because he maketh intercession for the saints according to the will of God.
verses.28: And we know that all things work together for good to them that love God, to them who are the called according to his purpose.
verses.29: For whom he did foreknow, he also did predestinate to be conformed to the image of his Son, that he might be the firstborn among many brethren.
verses.30: Moreover whom he did predestinate, them he also called: and whom he called, them he also justified: and whom he justified, them he also glorified.
verses.31: What shall we then say to these things? If God be for us, who can be against us?
verses.32: He that spared not his own Son, but delivered him up for us all, how shall he not with him also freely give us all things?
verses.33: Who shall lay any thing to the charge of God’s elect? It is God that justifieth.
verses.34: Who is he that condemneth? It is Christ that died, yea rather, that is risen again, who is even at the right hand of God, who also maketh intercession for us.
verses.35: Who shall separate us from the love of Christ? shall tribulation, or distress, or persecution, or famine, or nakedness, or peril, or sword?
verses.36: As it is written, For thy sake we are killed all the day long; we are accounted as sheep for the slaughter.
verses.37: Nay, in all these things we are more than conquerors through him that loved us.
verses.38: For I am persuaded, that neither death, nor life, nor angels, nor principalities, nor powers, nor things present, nor things to come,
verses.39: Nor height, nor depth, nor any other creature, shall be able to separate us from the love of God, which is in Christ Jesus our Lord.

verses.1: There is therefore now no condemnation to them that are in Christ Jesus.
verses.2: For the law of the Spirit of life in Christ Jesus made me free from the law of sin and of death.
verses.3: For what the law could not do, in that it was weak through the flesh, God, sending his own Son in the likeness of sinful flesh and for sin, condemned sin in the flesh:
verses.4: that the ordinance of the law might be fulfilled in us, who walk not after the flesh, but after the Spirit.
verses.5: For they that are after the flesh mind the things of the flesh; but they that are after the Spirit the things of the Spirit.
verses.6: For the mind of the flesh is death; but the mind of the Spirit is life and peace:
verses.7: because the mind of the flesh is enmity against God; for it is not subject to the law of God, neither indeed can it be:
verses.8: and they that are in the flesh cannot please God.
verses.9: But ye are not in the flesh but in the Spirit, if so be that the Spirit of God dwelleth in you. But if any man hath not the Spirit of Christ, he is none of his.
verses.10: And if Christ is in you, the body is dead because of sin; but the spirit is life because of righteousness.
verses.11: But if the Spirit of him that raised up Jesus from the dead dwelleth in you, he that raised up Christ Jesus from the dead shall give life also to your mortal bodies through his Spirit that dwelleth in you.
verses.12: So then, brethren, we are debtors, not to the flesh, to live after the flesh:
verses.13: for if ye live after the flesh, ye must die; but if by the Spirit ye put to death the deeds of the body, ye shall live.
verses.14: For as many as are led by the Spirit of God, these are sons of God.
verses.15: For ye received not the spirit of bondage again unto fear; but ye received the spirit of adoption, whereby we cry, Abba, Father.
verses.16: The Spirit himself beareth witness with our spirit, that we are children of God:
verses.17: and if children, then heirs; heirs of God, and joint-heirs with Christ; if so be that we suffer with him, that we may be also glorified with him.
verses.18: For I reckon that the sufferings of this present time are not worthy to be compared with the glory which shall be revealed to us-ward.
verses.19: For the earnest expectation of the creation waiteth for the revealing of the sons of God.
verses.20: For the creation was subjected to vanity, not of its own will, but by reason of him who subjected it, in hope
verses.21: that the creation itself also shall be delivered from the bondage of corruption into the liberty of the glory of the children of God.
verses.22: For we know that the whole creation groaneth and travaileth in pain together until now.
verses.23: And not only so, but ourselves also, who have the first-fruits of the Spirit, even we ourselves groan within ourselves, waiting for our adoption, to wit, the redemption of our body.
verses.24: For in hope were we saved: but hope that is seen is not hope: for who hopeth for that which he seeth?
verses.25: But if we hope for that which we see not, then do we with patience wait for it.
verses.26: And in like manner the Spirit also helpeth our infirmity: for we know not how to pray as we ought; but the Spirit himself maketh intercession for us with groanings which cannot be uttered;
verses.27: and he that searcheth the hearts knoweth what is the mind of the Spirit, because he maketh intercession for the saints according to the will of God.
verses.28: And we know that to them that love God all things work together for good, even to them that are called according to his purpose.
verses.29: For whom he foreknew, he also foreordained to be conformed to the image of his Son, that he might be the firstborn among many brethren:
verses.30: and whom he foreordained, them he also called: and whom he called, them he also justified: and whom he justified, them he also glorified.
verses.31: What then shall we say to these things? If God is for us, who is against us?
verses.32: He that spared not his own Son, but delivered him up for us all, how shall he not also with him freely give us all things?
verses.33: Who shall lay anything to the charge of God’s elect? It is God that justifieth;
verses.34: who is he that condemneth? It is Christ Jesus that died, yea rather, that was raised from the dead, who is at the right hand of God, who also maketh intercession for us.
verses.35: Who shall separate us from the love of Christ? shall tribulation, or anguish, or persecution, or famine, or nakedness, or peril, or sword?
verses.37: Nay, in all these things we are more than conquerors through him that loved us.
verses.38: For I am persuaded, that neither death, nor life, nor angels, nor principalities, nor things present, nor things to come, nor powers,
verses.39: nor height, nor depth, nor any other creature, shall be able to separate us from the love of God, which is in Christ Jesus our Lord.

verses.1: For this cause those who are in Christ Jesus will not be judged as sinners.
verses.2: For the law of the Spirit of life in Christ Jesus has made me free from the law of sin and death.
verses.3: For what the law was not able to do because it was feeble through the flesh, God, sending his Son in the image of the evil flesh, and as an offering for sin, gave his decision against sin in the flesh:
verses.4: So that what was ordered by the law might be done in us, who are living, not in the way of the flesh, but in the way of the Spirit.
verses.5: For those who are living in the way of the flesh give their minds to the things of the flesh, but those who go in the way of the Spirit, to the things of the Spirit.
verses.6: For the mind of the flesh is death, but the mind of the Spirit is life and peace:
verses.7: Because the mind of the flesh is opposite to God; it is not under the law of God, and is not able to be:
verses.8: So that those who are in the flesh are not able to give pleasure to God.
verses.9: You are not in the flesh but in the Spirit, if the Spirit of God is in you. But if any man has not the Spirit of Christ he is not one of his.
verses.10: And if Christ is in you, the body is dead because of sin, but the Spirit is life because of righteousness.
verses.11: But if the Spirit of him who made Jesus come again from the dead is in you, he who made Christ Jesus come again from the dead will in the same way, through his Spirit which is in you, give life to your bodies which now are under the power of death.
verses.12: So then, my brothers, we are in debt, not to the flesh to be living in the way of the flesh:
verses.13: For if you go in the way of the flesh, death will come on you; but if by the Spirit you put to death the works of the body, you will have life.
verses.14: And all those who are guided by the Spirit of God are sons of God.
verses.15: For you did not get the spirit of servants again to put you in fear, but the spirit of sons was given to you, by which we say, Abba, Father.
verses.16: The Spirit is witness with our spirit that we are children of God:
verses.17: And if we are children, we have a right to a part in the heritage; a part in the things of God, together with Christ; so that if we have a part in his pain, we will in the same way have a part in his glory.
verses.18: I am of the opinion that there is no comparison between the pain of this present time and the glory which we will see in the future.
verses.19: For the strong desire of every living thing is waiting for the revelation of the sons of God.
verses.20: For every living thing was put under the power of change, not by its desire, but by him who made it so, in hope
verses.21: That all living things will be made free from the power of death and will have a part with the free children of God in glory.
verses.22: For we are conscious that all living things are weeping and sorrowing in pain together till now.
verses.23: And not only so, but we who have the first fruits of the Spirit, even we have sorrow in our minds, waiting for the time when we will take our place as sons, that is, the salvation of our bodies.
verses.24: For our salvation is by hope: but hope which is seen is not hope: for who is hoping for what he sees?
verses.25: But if we have hope for that which we see not, then we will be able to go on waiting for it.
verses.26: And in the same way the Spirit is a help to our feeble hearts: for we are not able to make prayer to God in the right way; but the Spirit puts our desires into words which are not in our power to say;
verses.27: And he who is the searcher of hearts has knowledge of the mind of the Spirit, because he is making prayers for the saints in agreement with the mind of God.
verses.28: And we are conscious that all things are working together for good to those who have love for God, and have been marked out by his purpose.
verses.29: Because those of whom he had knowledge before they came into existence, were marked out by him to be made like his Son, so that he might be the first among a band of brothers:
verses.30: And those who were marked out by him were named; and those who were named were given righteousness; and to those to whom he gave righteousness, in the same way he gave glory.
verses.31: What may we say about these things? If God is for us, who is against us?
verses.32: He who did not keep back his only Son, but gave him up for us all, will he not with him freely give us all things?
verses.33: Who will say anything against the saints of God? It is God who makes us clear from evil;
verses.34: Who will give a decision against us? It is Christ Jesus who not only was put to death, but came again from the dead, who is now at the right hand of God, taking our part.
verses.35: Who will come between us and the love of Christ? Will trouble, or pain, or cruel acts, or the need of food or of clothing, or danger, or the sword?
verses.36: As it is said in the holy Writings, Because of you we are put to death every day; we are like sheep ready for destruction.
verses.37: But we are able to overcome all these things and more through his love.
verses.38: For I am certain that not death, or life, or angels, or rulers, or things present, or things to come, or powers,
verses.39: Or things on high, or things under the earth, or anything which is made, will be able to come between us and the love of God which is in Christ Jesus our Lord.

verses.2: For the law of the Spirit of life in Christ Jesus made me free from the law of sin and of death.
verses.3: For what the law couldn’t do, in that it was weak through the flesh, God did, sending his own Son in the likeness of sinful flesh and for sin, he condemned sin in the flesh,
verses.4: that the ordinance of the law might be fulfilled in us who don’t walk according to the flesh, but according to the Spirit.
verses.5: For those who live according to the flesh set their minds on the things of the flesh, but those who live according to the Spirit, the things of the Spirit.
verses.6: For the mind of the flesh is death, but the mind of the Spirit is life and peace;
verses.7: because the mind of the flesh is hostile toward God, for it is not subject to God’s law, neither indeed can it be.
verses.8: Those who are in the flesh can’t please God.
verses.9: But you are not in the flesh but in the Spirit, if it is so that the Spirit of God dwells in you. But if any man doesn’t have the Spirit of Christ, he is not his.
verses.10: If Christ is in you, the body is dead because of sin, but the spirit is alive because of righteousness.
verses.11: But if the Spirit of him who raised up Jesus from the dead dwells in you, he who raised up Christ Jesus from the dead will also give life to your mortal bodies through his Spirit who dwells in you.
verses.12: So then, brothers, we are debtors, not to the flesh, to live after the flesh.
verses.13: For if you live after the flesh, you must die; but if by the Spirit you put to death the deeds of the body, you will live.
verses.14: For as many as are led by the Spirit of God, these are children of God.
verses.16: The Spirit himself testifies with our spirit that we are children of God;
verses.17: and if children, then heirs—heirs of God and joint heirs with Christ, if indeed we suffer with him, that we may also be glorified with him.
verses.18: For I consider that the sufferings of this present time are not worthy to be compared with the glory which will be revealed toward us.
verses.19: For the creation waits with eager expectation for the children of God to be revealed.
verses.20: For the creation was subjected to vanity, not of its own will, but because of him who subjected it, in hope
verses.21: that the creation itself also will be delivered from the bondage of decay into the liberty of the glory of the children of God.
verses.22: For we know that the whole creation groans and travails in pain together until now.
verses.23: Not only so, but ourselves also, who have the first fruits of the Spirit, even we ourselves groan within ourselves, waiting for adoption, the redemption of our body.
verses.24: For we were saved in hope, but hope that is seen is not hope. For who hopes for that which he sees?
verses.25: But if we hope for that which we don’t see, we wait for it with patience.
verses.26: In the same way, the Spirit also helps our weaknesses, for we don’t know how to pray as we ought. But the Spirit himself makes intercession for us with groanings which can’t be uttered.
verses.27: He who searches the hearts knows what is on the Spirit’s mind, because he makes intercession for the saints according to God.
verses.28: We know that all things work together for good for those who love God, for those who are called according to his purpose.
verses.30: Whom he predestined, those he also called. Whom he called, those he also justified. Whom he justified, those he also glorified.
verses.31: What then shall we say about these things? If God is for us, who can be against us?
verses.32: He who didn’t spare his own Son, but delivered him up for us all, how would he not also with him freely give us all things?
verses.33: Who could bring a charge against God’s chosen ones? It is God who justifies.
verses.34: Who is he who condemns? It is Christ who died, yes rather, who was raised from the dead, who is at the right hand of God, who also makes intercession for us.
verses.35: Who shall separate us from the love of Christ? Could oppression, or anguish, or persecution, or famine, or nakedness, or peril, or sword?
verses.37: No, in all these things we are more than conquerors through him who loved us.
verses.38: For I am persuaded that neither death, nor life, nor angels, nor principalities, nor things present, nor things to come, nor powers,
verses.39: nor height, nor depth, nor any other created thing will be able to separate us from God’s love which is in Christ Jesus our Lord.
